name: Front
description: Drive Front (collaborative inbox) from any AI agent: conversations, messages, contacts, tags, inboxes, teammates. 13 tools, API-key Bearer auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the Front Core API (dev.frontapp.com/reference).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to Front → **Settings (top-right) → Developers → API Tokens → Create API Token**.
2. Pick scope: `private` (full-access to all inboxes/conversations) or scoped to a specific inbox/role.
3. Copy the token. Set `FRONT_API_TOKEN`.

**Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer ${FRONT_API_TOKEN}`.

**Front's tile model**:
  - **Conversation** = a thread between your team and one or more contacts. Has status (open/archived/spam/deleted), assignee, inbox, tags.
  - **Message** = each inbound/outbound email, SMS, chat, etc. within a conversation.
  - **Contact** = an external entity (a customer). Has handles[] of various types (email, phone, twitter, custom).
  - **Inbox** = a shared mailbox (could map to an email address, a Slack channel, an SMS number).
  - **Teammate** = a Front user.

**Sending replies**: a 'reply' is a new message in an existing conversation. POST `/conversations/{id}/messages` (the adapter wraps as `front_send_reply`). For new outbound conversations, POST `/channels/{id}/messages` (different endpoint).

**Pagination**: cursor-based via `?limit=N&page_token=...`. Response includes `_pagination.next` URL.

**Tagged dates**: dates on objects come as Unix timestamps (seconds, NOT ms).

**Resource references**: many fields are URLs like `https://api2.frontapp.com/contacts/cnt_xxx` instead of plain IDs — extract the ID after the last `/` if you need a bare ID, or just pass the URL back to Front in subsequent calls (Front accepts both).

**Rate limits**: ~50 req/sec per token (varies). On 429 honor headers.

**Out of scope here**: rules/automation, analytics endpoints, custom-channel creation, signature management, knowledge-base.

tools.name: front_me
tools.description: Return the API token's identity (id, name, email).

tools.name: front_list_inboxes
tools.description: List inboxes the token can access. Each has id, name, type (private/shared), send_as.

tools.name: front_list_conversations
tools.description: List conversations with optional filtering. Front's filter syntax: `q[statuses][]=open&q[statuses][]=archived&q[tag_ids][]=tag_xxx`.

q_statuses.description: Filter statuses: open, archived, spam, deleted.

tools.name: front_get_conversation
tools.description: Fetch a single conversation including assignee, tags, recipient handles, links to messages/comments.

tools.name: front_list_conversation_messages
tools.description: List all messages in a conversation chronologically.

tools.name: front_send_reply
tools.description: Send a reply (new message) in an existing conversation. Required: body. Sender is the API token's identity unless `author_id` overrides.

body.description: Reply body (HTML for email, plain for SMS/chat).

author_id.description: Teammate ID — send AS this person (impersonation, requires admin scope).

channel_id.description: Channel to send through (if conversation has multiple).

tools.name: front_add_comment
tools.description: Add an internal comment (private note) to a conversation. Only visible to teammates.

tools.name: front_update_conversation
tools.description: Update conversation status, assignee, or tags. PATCH semantics — only pass what to change.

assignee_id.description: Teammate ID to assign (or null to unassign).

tools.name: front_get_contact
tools.description: Fetch a contact with all handles[] (email/phone/twitter/custom).

contactId.description: Contact ID (starts with 'cnt_') OR handle URL like 'alt:email:a@b.com'.

tools.name: front_create_contact
tools.description: Create a contact. Required: handles (at least one).

handles.description: [{handle:'a@b.com', source:'email'}] — source can be email, phone, twitter, custom, etc.

links.description: Array of URL strings (e.g. their CRM record).
